token.name: The Black Bull
token.supply: 999961749.112082 (ui amount; ~1B nominal; mint & freeze authority renounced; Token-2022 program TokenzQdBNbLqP5VEhdkAS6EPFLC1PHnBqCXEpPxuEb)

pumpfun_metadata.name: The Black Bull
pumpfun_metadata.creator: yHCxHBEaJW5tbndqC8JciSThr7U1cqLpdcsvHcx6PRe
pumpfun_metadata.notes: Onchain Token-2022 metadata reads name='The Black Bull', symbol='ANSEM'. CoinGecko id 'the-black-bull' (rank ~333) lists exactly this contract. DexScreener info block adds later community socials: website https://www.blackbullsol.com/, X https://x.com/blackbullsol, Telegram https://t.me/blackbullsolana. IMPORTANT: token created by an anonymous deployer, NOT by Ansem — deployer spent ~$6.3K, bought 792.45M tokens, sent 650M (~65% of supply) to Ansem's wallet, sold remaining 142.45M for ~$11.8K, netting ~$5.5K (Lookonchain).

copycats.verdict: CONFIRMED copycat/decoy — NOT a migrated official pool
copycats.created: ~2026-06-27/28 (its single Raydium CLMM pool created 2026-06-28T01:43:27Z, during the real token's breakout)
evidence: Classic SPL token (real ANSEM is Token-2022 via pump.fun); no matching metadata; no CoinGecko listing
Only 1,143 token accounts total (real mint has thousands, >3,000 sampled without exhausting)
Supply 999,960,532 chosen to mimic the real mint's supply; even mirrors the real token's ~58% top-holder profile

99.96% of supply in two accounts: 582.85M (58.3%) in Raydium CLMM pool vault EXH9QTUmK4PRKMd2xJfLqy3iZoKXxLov4JKmaPpUuxmT and 416.13M (41.6%) in deployer wallet DdoCXsaRqk1aS2TVjGxeE6yTMX2bmC5hhcsqwAyuVNBT

The '$86M liquidity' (DexScreener claims $86,489,365) is one-sided CLMM depth denominated almost entirely in the copycat token itself — only 0.1362 USDC on the quote side; 24h volume $3.35 from 29 txns
All other holders have dust balances (3k-6k tokens), typical of spam/bait airdrops

copycats.verdict: Press (The Defiant, Crypto Times, Coinsprobe/MEXC, crypto.news, Crypto Briefing) uniformly warn multiple ANSEM-named variants trade on Solana and buyers of wrong contracts lost money instantly; unrelated CoinGecko-listed lookalikes include 'Ansems Dog', 'Ansem's Cat', 'ANSEM ARMY'

market_snapshot.liquidity: ~$4.55M real USD liquidity across 30 DEX pairs (top: PumpSwap ANSEM/SOL $1.68M, Meteora ANSEM/SOL $1.42M + $0.64M + $0.43M, Meteora ANSEM/USDC $0.15M). The $86M 'liquidity' figure belongs to the copycat BWVL pool and is spoofed one-sided depth.
market_snapshot.volume_24h: ~$31-36M organic (GeckoTerminal $31.25M; CoinGecko $35.53M incl. CEX tickers e.g. MEXC ANSEM/USDT ~$1.23M; DexScreener's $69.3M sum includes an unreliable $11.7M Orca HYPE/ANSEM pair on ~$10K liquidity)

ansem.bio: Real name Zion Thomas. BS Computer Science, Georgia Tech (~2017); software engineer at Capital One; amateur boxer; entered crypto 2017; Head of Research at TCG Crypto (blockchain arm of The Chernin Group) per Phemex. Known as 'King of Solana Memes'.
ansem.followers: ~1M-1.07M (press 'close to 1 million'; task context 1.07M; Phemex says >750,000 — likely stale)
ansem.background: Famous for calling SOL near $1.50-$8 pre-rally (2023: $8 to ~$300), Dogwifhat/WIF early (~520x reported; called at $100K mcap, peaked $4.8B), ~80x on BONK. Estimated net worth ~$20-30M (2026 trackers). Controversies: Oct 2024 ZachXBT accusation of promoting low-cap memecoins (BODEN, HOBBES, ZEUS, WYNN — all later down >98%) in pump-and-dump-like fashion; unproven, no formal findings; Ansem admitted supporting some celebrity memecoins 'was a mistake'. His identity was used in a 2024 impersonation phishing scam (~$2.5M victim losses). He did NOT create $ANSEM: on 2026-06-16 he tweeted he had linked his X to a pump.fun account and was 'not endorsing any microcaps'; an anonymous deployer sent ~65% of supply to his wallet, and he later leaned in, pledging creator-fee airdrops.

timeline.event: ZachXBT publicly accuses Ansem of promoting low-cap Solana memecoins (BODEN, HOBBES, ZEUS, WYNN — all later down >98%) to his then-500K+ followers; Ansem rejects it, citing his WIF call ($100K mcap → $4.8B peak). Unproven, no formal findings.
Ansem tweets he linked his X to a pump.fun account: 'the other one is not me... im not endorsing any microcaps' — initially distancing himself.

$ANSEM 'The Black Bull' (9cRC...pump) created on pump.fun by anonymous wallet yHCxHB...; bonds and migrates to PumpSwap pool FnzKY6.... Deployer spends ~$6.3K, buys 792.45M tokens, sends 650M (~65%) to Ansem's wallet, sells 142.45M for ~$11.8K, nets ~$5.5K. (Press said 'around June 17'; onchain timestamp is June 16.)
Token trades quietly at $0.0002-0.0008 (sub-$1M FDV); all-time low $0.0001441 on 2026-06-25T10:24Z.
@Clive_99 tweets that Ansem should airdrop creator fees to holders — the suggestion Ansem quote-tweets the next day.

CATALYST: Ansem announces he will airdrop pump.fun creator fees ('retweet + follow me on there + comment with your pump profile & ill pick randomly weekly'). $ANSEM rallies from ~$173K to $8.6M mcap same day; Meteora pools spin up.
Main airdrop campaign: ~$6.7-7M of $ANSEM sent to 700+ wallets (min ~$23 each), funded by creator fees; goal to grow holders from ~25K to 1M.
Copycat BWVL mint's single Raydium CLMM pool created during the breakout, with spoofed one-sided '$86M liquidity' to bait mistaken buyers.

Ansem tweets 'had to give the trenches a stimmy since pump refuses to' as first airdrop rounds land. Same day: Ash Crypto notes $173K→$109M mcap in 24h (630x) 'in the middle of the bear market'; $ANSEM tops pump.fun 24h volume ($60M); Lookonchain flags the 261x trader; Ansem tweets copycat coins 'should airdrop to $ANSEM holders'; mcap reported ~$66M.

Ansem tweets: airdropped ~$7M so far, will do more as mcap rises; goal 1M holders vs ~25K now. Same day: press-cycle ATH ~$0.121-0.129 (FDV briefly >$120M, 24h vol $80-91M); Bubblemaps posts distribution tiers and ~60% Ansem concentration; Lookonchain shows deployer made only $5.5K; Arkham: Ansem up $75M, 'ansemconzimp' sent 65% of supply, 58.7% remaining after airdrops; ANSEM perps launch on Aster DEX (5x); The Defiant publishes.

CONCENTRATION EXPOSED: Lookonchain reports 67.38M ANSEM (~$9.43M) airdropped to 704 wallets, with 49.89M (~74%, $6.98M) to just 7 wallets that already sold 38.29M for $1.29M; 26.6% of recipients fully liquidated. Crypto Times and MEXC publish critical coverage; token trades ~$0.136-0.14.

Pump.fun records highest daily launches/graduations in 80 days, driven by $ANSEM, reversing a 3-month ~80% activity slump; multiple ANSEM-named variants proliferate; begging culture peaks (e.g. SuiLFG 'letter to Ansem'); price closes ~$0.155.

New all-time high $0.179815 (~12:00 UTC; ATH mcap ~$182M / FDV basis), surpassing the June 29 peak. As of ~13:52 UTC: price ~$0.1766, circulating mcap ~$73.1M, FDV ~$176.6M, real DEX liquidity ~$4.55M, 24h volume ~$31-36M.

open_questions: Is GV6UUmNxz2RpKxmNAPadYKb7uQpszwqQAu3qLJxVdC52 definitively Ansem's wallet? Onchain holdings (584.27M = 58.43%) match Arkham's 58.7% and press '~604M/60%' pre-airdrop figures closely, but no source published the base58 address with an explicit Ansem attribution; treat as strong inference.
Who are the 7 wallets that received ~74% of the airdrop and dumped? Lookonchain never published their addresses; whether they are insiders, sybil clusters, or engagement-farm winners is unknown.

Who deployed the token? yHCxHBEaJW5tbndqC8JciSThr7U1cqLpdcsvHcx6PRe is anonymous; @Supermanonchain's self-claim (tweet 2071581476207182216) is unverified. Any coordination with Ansem pre-launch is unproven.

Airdrop total conflict: $6.7M (Bubblemaps 06-29) vs ~$7M (Ansem 06-29) vs $9.43M (Lookonchain 06-30). Likely different snapshots/rounds, but exact per-round amounts and full recipient list were never published.

The 'reviving the bull market' framing has no literal primary source — exhaustive tweet searches found only the 'stimmy' tweet and mcap-contingent airdrop pledge; Ash Crypto explicitly framed the pump as happening in a bear market.

Market cap discrepancies ($43M vs $66M vs $125M+ across press) stem from circulating-vs-FDV confusion; The Defiant flagged this explicitly. Circulating supply (~415.7M) vs Ansem's ~58% holding also don't fully reconcile across aggregators.
Ansem follower count: 750K (Phemex, likely stale) vs ~1M (most press) vs 1.07M (task context).
Was the BWVL copycat pool created to bait buyers specifically, and by whom (DdoCXsaRqk1aS2TVjGxeE6yTMX2bmC5hhcsqwAyuVNBT)? Motive and operator unidentified.

Sustainability: weekly creator-fee airdrops (~$200-378K/week) vs the ~$7-9.4M already distributed implies most of the airdrop came from token holdings, not just fees — the exact funding split was never itemized.
Press launch-date conflict: 'around June 17' (Lookonchain via Defiant/Crypto Times) vs onchain 2026-06-16T21:05:48Z — resolved in favor of onchain (June 16).
